title: The strategic competition with China: winning the multi-domain race
summary: The article discusses the strategic competition with China, emphasizing the need to win the multi-domain race in military innovation. It highlights how historical military advancements have reshaped warfare and rendered past strategies obsolete.

title: Relativity Space to expand Terran R production in Florida
summary: Relativity Space has partnered with Space Florida to establish manufacturing and test facilities for its Terran R rocket on Florida’s Space Coast. This expansion aims to boost the company's production capabilities for the upcoming rocket.

title: Artemis 2 astronaut advocates for equatorial lunar landings
summary: The pilot of NASA's Artemis 2 mission suggests prioritizing human landings in equatorial regions of the Moon for the initial return, rather than targeting the more challenging south pole.

title: Gravity-1 sea launch off Shanghai puts 9 satellites into orbit
summary: Orienspace successfully launched its third Gravity-1 solid rocket, deploying nine satellites into orbit from a sea launch off Shanghai. The 30-meter-long rocket marks another milestone for the commercial launch firm.

title: Zenno Astronautics to relocate to the United States
summary: Zenno Astronautics, a New Zealand startup specializing in superconducting magnets for spacecraft applications, plans to relocate its headquarters to the United States. The move aims to enhance the company's growth and operational capabilities in the space industry.

title: SpaceX launches Northrop mission to extend the life of aging satellites
summary: SpaceX launched a Northrop Grumman mission to extend the lifespan of aging satellites by installing propulsion pods. The DARPA-backed vehicle will service three commercial satellites in geostationary orbit.

title: Northrop adds to charges on Vulcan solid rocket motor program
summary: Northrop Grumman incurred additional charges for the solid rocket boosters used in the Vulcan Centaur program and indicated that updated motors may not be ready until the end of the year.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Three New Associate Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) has announced the addition of three new associate members: Overview Energy, Loft Orbital, and Star Catcher. These companies are involved in space-based energy and other space industry advancements.

title: UK and Florida commit $400,000 to joint space projects
summary: The UK and Florida have each pledged $200,000 to fund joint space research, innovation, and commercialization projects. This collaboration aims to strengthen ties between the UK and Florida's aerospace sectors.

title: German component supplier deltaVision raises 10.2 million euros
summary: German spacecraft component developer deltaVision raised 10.2 million euros ($11.6 million) to expand production of valves and related equipment, focusing on enabling in-space refueling.

title: 'The court has to intervene now': Indigenous groups and conservationists condemn SpaceX's South Texas land swap
summary: Indigenous groups and conservationists have filed an emergency injunction to block a land swap between SpaceX and the federal government, arguing that courts should assess its legality. The move aims to halt SpaceX's operations in South Texas amid environmental and cultural concerns.

title: New timelapse video shows NASA's Psyche spacecraft zooming by Mars
summary: A new timelapse video showcases NASA's Psyche spacecraft passing by Mars. The mission provided unique data that complemented existing Mars science despite limited anticipated discoveries.

title: Metal spheres found in Australia show how little we still know about falling space debris, experts say
summary: Metal spheres found in Australia highlight the uncertainties surrounding falling space debris. Experts emphasize the lack of knowledge about the behavior of rockets and other debris upon re-entry.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers the secrets of cosmic 'factories' that filled the early universe with stardust
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has uncovered the secrets of early galaxies that produced stardust in the young universe. By studying a nearby modern galaxy, astronomers gained insights into these cosmic 'factories'.

title: Alien life likely can't survive on exoplanets smaller than Mars, scientists say
summary: Scientists suggest that exoplanets smaller than Mars are unlikely to sustain alien life due to their inability to maintain a stable atmosphere. The study indicates that a planetary body needs to be at least as massive as Mars to support life.

title: Space Force orders 36 more Golden Dome missile-tracking satellites, for $1.75 billion
summary: The Space Development Agency awarded contracts worth up to $1.75 billion to two companies for the development of 36 Golden Dome missile-tracking satellites as part of a missile-defense initiative.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket sends Starlink satellites to orbit a day after rare launch abort
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket successfully launched 24 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on July 21, following a rare abort of its first liftoff attempt the previous day.

title: Artemis III astronauts will beam 4k video from Orion to Earth using SpaceX Starlink lasers
summary: NASA will use SpaceX's Starlink laser communications terminals on the Artemis III Orion spacecraft to transmit 4K video and data-heavy mission downlinks to Earth. This partnership aims to enhance high-definition video transmissions during the mission.

title: A Week of Smoky Skies Across North America
summary: Wildland fires in July 2026 emitted brown carbon, significantly worsening air quality across parts of the U.S. and Canada over a week. The event highlighted the impact of wildfires on atmospheric conditions.

title: NISAR’s L-Band Radar Reveals ‘Hummingbird’ in Antarctica
summary: NISAR's L-band radar captured an image of Nunatak Zaterjavshijsja in East Antarctica, showing how the mountaintop disrupts ice flow and causes fracturing. The U.S.-India satellite mission continues to provide detailed Earth observation data.

title: NASA to Host Media Briefing on Roman Telescope, Launching Next Month
summary: NASA will host a media briefing on the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope mission on July 29, ahead of its planned launch on August 30 from Kennedy Space Center. The event will be livestreamed to the public.

title: US-India Satellite Delivers Data, Reveals ‘Hummingbird’ in Antarctica
summary: The NISAR satellite, a joint NASA-ISRO mission, has begun delivering public data from its radar instruments, revealing new insights like the 'Hummingbird' feature in Antarctica. The satellite's L-band and S-band radars will provide ongoing data to track Earth's movements.

title: NASA Langley Celebrates Community through Music with ‘Symphony Under the Stars’ Event
summary: NASA Langley hosted 'Symphony Under the Stars' to celebrate its community and the 250th anniversary of the U.S. with music and performances. The event brought together employees, families, and local residents.

title: NIAC 2026 Selections
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory and the University of Central Florida have selected three projects for the 2026 NIAC Phase I, including DimSun for solar dimming via dust clouds and CANVAS for Venus mobility, alongside PS21 for space interferometry.

title: Precision Astrometry Using Optically Independent Spacecraft for Graviational Wave Detection
summary: A new method for detecting gravitational waves at low frequencies using precision astrometry with optically independent spacecraft has been proposed. The innovation aims to observe coordinated apparent motions of celestial objects caused by gravitational waves passing Earth.

title: Mapping Alien Continents: Achieving Optical VLBI for Exoplanet Imaging
summary: A new study proposes achieving optical Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) to image Earth-like exoplanets in visible light. The innovation involves a 'dynamic hierarchical nulling' interferometer design to combine inputs and resolve surface features.

title: Interworld Slingshot Resource Surveys
summary: A proposal by Interworld Slots and Pablo Sobron Sanchez from the SETI Institute suggests using Raman spectroscopy on high-speed spacecraft to map minerals during flybys of the Moon, asteroids, and Mars' moons. This concept could help NASA assess resources like ice, ilmenite, and volatile-bearing minerals without landing or sample return.

title: Robotically Assembled Electromagnetic Metamaterials for Long-Range Space Situational Awareness
summary: Researchers at Duke University are developing robotically assembled electromagnetic metamaterials to enhance long-range space situational awareness (SSA) for tracking smaller space objects. This technology aims to overcome limitations of ground-based systems like the US space surveillance network.

title: Falcon 9 to launch MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is scheduled to launch the MRV-1 robotic servicing spacecraft for Northrop Grumman. This mission aims to demonstrate in-space servicing capabilities.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Starship, and Chinese rockets scheduled to launch
summary: SpaceX is preparing for a new attempt of Starship Flight 13 after last week's aborted launch. Additionally, Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets are also scheduled to launch.

title: Making Progress: Relativity’s Terran R, Rocket Lab’s Neutron, and PLD Space’s Miura 5
summary: Relativity Space, Rocket Lab, and PLD Space are making progress on their new rocket developments: Terran R, Neutron, and Miura 5, respectively. While Starship's test program has been in the spotlight, these companies continue advancing their launch vehicle projects.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13 was aborted during ignition due to Booster 20 failing to ignite properly. The launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT was unsuccessful.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for its first land recovery.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. This marks another crewed mission to the orbiting laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS) this week, while SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are planned globally.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20's 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in the company's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Plato's electronics ready for space
summary: The European Space Agency's PLATO spacecraft has completed key electromagnetic compatibility tests in ESTEC's Maxwell Test Chamber, confirming its electronics are fit for space. PLATO is now cleared for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket, scheduled for March 2027.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm technology is being developed for space applications, aiming to revolutionize satellite servicing and maintenance. This innovation could significantly reduce costs and extend the lifespan of orbital assets.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its main instrument, ASPIICS, are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The UK Scraps the Department in Charge of UKSA
summary: The UK has decided to abolish the department overseeing the UK Space Agency (UKSA) as highlighted during the Farnborough Air Show. New Prime Minister Andy Burnham took the opportunity to dismantle the department.

title: The UK and Space Florida Launch Joint Fund
summary: The UK and Space Florida have launched a joint fund to support projects with partners from both regions. This initiative aims to foster collaboration between the UK and Florida's space sectors.

title: Breaking Down the $686B Space Economy
summary: The global space economy reached a record $686 billion in 2025, as reported by the Space Foundation. This marks significant growth in the industry.

title: FCC Eyes Wi-Fi and Bluetooth Bands for Satellites
summary: The FCC is considering a proposal to permit unlicensed wireless devices to communicate directly with satellites using Wi-Fi and Bluetooth bands. This move aims to expand satellite connectivity options for consumer devices.

title: deltaVision Raises €10.2M to Build In-Orbit Refueling Tech
summary: deltaVision has raised €10.2 million to develop in-orbit refueling technology. The funds will be used to expand manufacturing capacity and research new capabilities.

title: UK Commits £62M into Domestic Space Capabilities
summary: The UK government allocated £62 million ($83.3 million) to boost domestic satellite communications and space technologies. This investment aims to enhance the UK's space capabilities.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket, becoming the first private Indian company to reach orbit. The mission took off from India's Satish Dhawan Space Centre.

title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense increased its national security space launch contracts ceiling by $11.4 billion, tripling the total pool from $5.6 billion to $17 billion.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs secured a $10 million seed funding round and relocated to New York City. The company focuses on addressing size, weight, and power constraints in spaceflight.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has awarded SpaceX a contract to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission using Starlink. This partnership aims to enhance communication capabilities during the lunar mission.

title: Contrivian Completes Merger With Big Network to Fuel Orchestration Service
summary: Contrivian has finalized its merger with Big Network to create a vertically integrated, multi-modal connectivity platform. The deal aims to enhance orchestration services in the space industry.

title: SpaceX Launches Northrop Grumman’s First Mission Robotic Vehicle
summary: SpaceX successfully launched Northrop Grumman's first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV-MEP) for on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension. The mission marks a significant step in satellite servicing and in-space logistics.

title: Arianespace Sets Date for Fourth Ariane 6 Launch This Year
summary: Arianespace has scheduled the fourth Ariane 6 launch of the year for August 27, aiming to deploy the second Meteosat Third Generation Imager satellite (MTG-I2). This mission highlights Arianespace's continued efforts to expand its launch cadence and support weather observation capabilities.

title: Poland Commits Nearly $750M to Europe’s IRIS² Constellation
summary: Poland has committed 656 million euro ($748 million) to Europe's IRIS² satellite constellation, covering nearly 10% of its funding. The agreement was signed in Warsaw.

title: UK Government to Invest Further Millions to Boost Space Sector
summary: The UK government plans to invest an additional £62 million ($83.4 million) to strengthen the country's space sector, focusing on new satellite communications systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Makes History for India With First Launch Success
summary: Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched its Vikram-1 rocket, marking India's first private orbital launch. The mission achieved orbit, making it a historic milestone for the country's space industry.

title: ThinKom ThinAir GT Antenna Secures Government Certification for O3b mPower
summary: ThinKom's ThinAir GT2517 terminal has become the first airborne antenna to receive SES's Government Technology Certification for use on the O3b mPOWER constellation. This certification enables government operations on SES's satellite network.

title: Spire Hires Mantech Exec Eric Mellinger as COO
summary: Spire Global has appointed Eric Mellinger, a former Mantech International executive, as its new COO to oversee business development and go-to-market strategy. Mellinger brings nearly eight years of experience from his tenure at Mantech.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to collaborate on lunar exploration, focusing on technical information exchange and system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline with Lockheed Martin.
